title: aster novae-angliae f. spurius
intro: Aster novae-angliae f. spurius, commonly known as New England aster, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to alleviate symptoms of fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, while also promoting wound healing and reducing pain. As a natural remedy, New England aster has been used to treat colds, sore throats, and skin irritations, showcasing its potential health benefits when used appropriately.
